user_story: As a subscriber using Siri / App Intents, I want a deterministic, instant snapshot of my current state so that Siri can speak it aloud without waking my container.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/siri/status/ requires JWT authentication (IsAuthenticated). Returns 404 {"error": "no_tenant"} if the user has no tenant. On success, calls render_context_digest (same source as ChatContextView) capped at 4000 chars, PII-rehydrates real names from tenant.pii_entity_map (fail-open on rehydrate error), and returns {"snapshot_md": <markdown string>, "generated_at": <ISO timestamp>} with Cache-Control: no-store. No LLM call; no container wake; no per-tenant model budget consumed. Rate-limited at 60 requests/minute per user (SiriStatusMinuteThrottle).
inventory_notes: PII rehydrate failure is non-fatal and logged; the unrehydrated snapshot is still returned. The 4000-char cap is bounded by CONTEXT_DIGEST_MIN/MAX_CHARS from workspace_envelope.

feature: Siri Fast Responder with Escalation (Tier 2 / Tier 3)
user_story: As a subscriber using Siri, I want a spoken answer within a few seconds so that simple questions are answered instantly, and complex questions are routed to my full assistant.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/siri/respond/ requires JWT auth. Body: {"intent": "...", "client_msg_id": "<optional>"}. Validates: non-empty intent (400 empty_intent), intent <= 1000 chars (400 intent_too_long), client_msg_id <= 64 chars (400 invalid_client_msg_id). Builds a 4000-char PII-rehydrated snapshot, then calls chat_completion with platform OpenRouter ZDR key using models [deepseek-v4-flash, deepseek-v4-pro] at 8s timeout / 300 max tokens. If the fast model replies without the [[ESCALATE]] sentinel, returns {"answered": true, "text": "<spoken answer>"} with no persistence. If the model returns [[ESCALATE]] (leading, case-insensitive), is empty, or throws, escalates: creates AppChatMessage + PendingMessage via enqueue_tenant_turn (budget-gated via check_budget — over-budget turns get status=error/error=budget_exhausted and no container wake), returns {"answered": false, "escalated": true, "client_msg_id": "<id>", "turn": {...}}. Escalation is idempotent on client_msg_id. Rate-limited at 30/minute (SiriRespondMinuteThrottle). All responses have Cache-Control: no-store.
dependencies: apps/common/openrouter.chat_completion (OpenRouter ZDR key), apps/router/chat_views.enqueue_tenant_turn, apps/router/chat_views.check_budget, PostgreSQL (AppChatMessage, PendingMessage, ChatThread)
inventory_notes: Stray mid-reply mixed-case [[escalate]] sentinel (not at start) is stripped from the answer, not spoken. Model errors and transport failures all fall through to escalation rather than returning an error to the user. The fast model cost is platform-absorbed (not per-tenant quota). Sentinel trailing content (e.g. '[[ESCALATE]] needs calendar tool') still escalates.

user_story: As a subscriber on iOS, I want to register my device's APNs token so that the assistant can push me a notification when my reply is ready.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/push/register/ requires JWT auth. Body: {"device_token": "<hex>", "environment": "production|sandbox", "bundle_id": "<optional>"}. Validates token as hex 32–200 chars (400 invalid_token), rejects missing tenant (404 no_tenant). Upserts a DeviceToken row keyed on the token string (globally unique constraint): re-points the token to the registering user atomically via update_or_create, absorbing concurrent retries via the unique constraint (the same token can be re-registered to a new user on account switch/device handoff without a delete+create race). Unknown environment values are silently normalized to 'production'. Returns {"registered": true} 200. No rate limit.
inventory_notes: Token is globally unique: registering re-points the token to the new user — a prior user's row is overwritten, preventing cross-user push. No explicit 201/200 distinction: all successful registrations return 200.

user_story: As a subscriber on iOS, I want to unregister my APNs device token on logout or app uninstall so that I stop receiving push notifications for that device.
expected_behaviour: DELETE /api/v1/push/register/ requires JWT auth. Token is read from query param ?device_token= first; if absent, from request body. Returns 400 invalid_token if no token provided. Deletes DeviceToken rows matching (user=request.user, token=<token>), so the caller can only delete their own tokens. Returns 204 No Content on success (even if no row existed — silent no-op).
inventory_notes: The query-param fallback handles clients whose HTTP stack sends DELETE with no body. Scoped to request.user: cannot delete another user's token.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to send a test push notification to my own devices so I can verify end-to-end APNs delivery is working.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/push/test/ requires JWT auth. If APNs is not configured (missing APNS_AUTH_KEY/KEY_ID/TEAM_ID/BUNDLE_ID), returns {"sent": 0, "failed": 0, "skipped": "not_configured"} 200. If user has no registered tokens, returns {"skipped": "no_tokens"} 200. Otherwise, fans out to all registered tokens grouped by environment (sandbox vs production), sending the fixed static body "Test push — notifications are working." (no user data in payload). APNs tokens reported as unregistered (410 / BadDeviceToken) are pruned from DeviceToken. Returns {"sent": N, "failed": N, "unregistered": N} 200. Rate-limited at 10/minute per user (PushTestMinuteThrottle). Never returns user PII or token values in the response.
dependencies: apps/common/apns.send_push, apps/common/apns.apns_configured, PostgreSQL (DeviceToken), Apple APNs HTTP/2 API, httpx[http2] package
inventory_notes: If the h2 package is not installed, send_push returns skipped=http2_unavailable. The test push cannot be directed at any specific device — it always targets all devices registered to the JWT user, so it cannot push to another user's device.

feature: APNs Reply-Ready Push (iOS Chat Turn Completion)
user_story: As the system, I want to push a notification to the user's iOS device when a chat turn completes so that the user is notified without reopening the app.
expected_behaviour: Called internally by apps/router/pending_queue.py after _drain_ios_batch completes a turn. notify_app_reply_ready(tenant, client_msg_ids, reply_text) claims notified_at on AppChatMessage rows (atomic isnull→now UPDATE) — only the first claim pushes, re-drains are no-ops. Strips markdown to a 140-char plain-text preview (or falls back to generic body for tables/empty replies). Routes to each device grouped by environment, sends with collapse_id=client_msg_id and content_available=True (hybrid alert + background wake). APNs 410/BadDeviceToken tokens are pruned. notify_app_reply_error sends a fixed error body with no diagnostic reason. Both are fail-open — any exception is logged, not re-raised. Short-circuits if APNs is not configured.
dependencies: apps/common/apns.send_push, PostgreSQL (AppChatMessage.notified_at, DeviceToken), Apple APNs HTTP/2 API
inventory_notes: Idempotency is dual-layer: server-side atomic notified_at claim prevents double-push on QStash retry; device-side apns-collapse-id coalesces multiple pushes for the same turn. content_available=1 triggers background fetch which iOS may throttle or suppress on force-quit/Background App Refresh off.

user_story: As the system, I want to push a notification to the user's iOS device when a cron job or proactive send completes so that iOS-only users are notified without Telegram/LINE.
expected_behaviour: Called by apps/router/proactive_context.py after recording a ProactiveOutbound row. notify_proactive_ready(tenant, proactive_id, body_source) claims ProactiveOutbound.notified_at atomically (isnull→now UPDATE); only the first claim pushes. Resolves the recipient from tenant.user (no User FK on ProactiveOutbound). Finds the tenant's main ChatThread id to set thread-id in the push payload. Sends with collapse_id='cron:<proactive_id>', content_available=True, extra={"id": "cron:<id>", "source": "cron"}. Routes per device environment, prunes stale tokens. Fail-open: any exception is logged but not re-raised. Short-circuits if APNs is not configured or proactive_id is falsy.
dependencies: apps/common/apns.send_push, PostgreSQL (ProactiveOutbound.notified_at, DeviceToken, ChatThread), Apple APNs HTTP/2 API
inventory_notes: The push body is a glanceable PII-safe taste; the authoritative text reaches the app via GET /chat/messages/?since= feed (the cron:<id> row). The 'app' channel on ProactiveOutbound covers iOS-only users who have no Telegram/LINE chat_id.

feature: APNs Provider JWT Signing and Caching
user_story: As the system, I want to mint and cache a short-lived ES256 provider JWT for APNs so that push requests are authenticated without re-signing on every send.
expected_behaviour: apps/common/apns._provider_jwt() signs a JWT with APNS_TEAM_ID as issuer using APNS_AUTH_KEY (ES256 private key, .p8 format) and caches it module-level for up to 3000 seconds (50 minutes, inside Apple's 1h validity window). On cache miss or expiry, re-signs via PyJWT. send_push() calls this before every request batch; if signing fails, returns {"skipped": "jwt_error"} without raising. send_push() also requires httpx[http2] — without the h2 package, returns {"skipped": "http2_unavailable"}. APNs is fully gated behind apns_configured() which checks all four env vars.
dependencies: PyJWT library, httpx[http2] library, Apple APNs API (api.push.apple.com / api.sandbox.push.apple.com), settings: APNS_AUTH_KEY, APNS_KEY_ID, APNS_TEAM_ID, APNS_BUNDLE_ID, APNS_USE_SANDBOX
inventory_notes: Module-level JWT cache (_jwt_cache) is process-local — multiple container replicas each maintain their own cache (not shared via Redis). APNS_USE_SANDBOX global controls sandbox fallback when per-device environment not explicitly passed. Token truncated to 64 bytes for apns-collapse-id per Apple spec.

feature: Core AI On-Device Model Manifest Endpoint
user_story: As an iOS subscriber, I want to fetch the on-device model manifest so my app can download and verify the correct model bundle files.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/coreai/model/manifest/ requires JWT auth (IsAuthenticated). Returns 404 {"detail": "No on-device model is configured."} if settings.COREAI_MODEL_BASE_URL or settings.COREAI_MODEL_MANIFEST_PATH are unset, or if the manifest file does not exist at the path. Returns 503 {"detail": "Model manifest unavailable."} if the manifest file is present but cannot be parsed (OSError, ValueError, KeyError, TypeError) — fail-closed so a corrupt manifest is never served to clients that verify sha256. On success returns {"name", "version", "total_bytes", "files": [{"path", "url", "sha256", "bytes"}, ...]} where each URL is constructed as <COREAI_MODEL_BASE_URL>/<name>/<version>/<percent-encoded-path>. total_bytes is taken from the manifest if present, otherwise summed from file entries.
dependencies: settings.COREAI_MODEL_BASE_URL (Azure Blob / CDN), settings.COREAI_MODEL_MANIFEST_PATH (local filesystem path to JSON), local filesystem
inventory_notes: The manifest JSON is read from disk on every request (no caching). File URLs use per-segment URL encoding (quote() per slash-separated part), not whole-path encoding. The 404 path allows the iOS app to gracefully fall back to Apple's on-device model (iOS 26) without user-facing error.

feature: Core AI Manifest Generation Management Command
user_story: As an operator, I want to generate a manifest JSON from an exported CoreML LanguageBundle directory so that the manifest endpoint serves the correct file list with verified sha256 checksums.
expected_behaviour: python manage.py generate_coreai_manifest --bundle <dir> --name <name> --model-version <ver> --out <path>. Walks the bundle directory recursively, skipping .DS_Store files. For each file computes streaming sha256 (1MB chunks) and byte size. Writes JSON {"name", "version", "total_bytes", "files": [{"path", "sha256", "bytes"}]} to the --out path (creates parent dirs). Exits with CommandError if the bundle dir is missing or contains no files. On success prints a message with file count, total GB, and the expected upload path pattern. --version is reserved by BaseCommand; the model version arg is --model-version.
inventory_notes: Files are sorted by rglob before hashing, giving a deterministic ordering. The operator must separately upload the bundle files to <COREAI_MODEL_BASE_URL>/<name>/<model_version>/<path> (e.g. Azure Blob). --version flag name collision with BaseCommand is the reason --model-version is used.

feature: Chat Turn Progress Narration (Internal Runtime Callback)
user_story: As the OpenClaw container runtime, I want to report coarse activity phases (waking/thinking/tool/composing) to the control plane so the iOS polling client can show live progress instead of a spinner.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/internal/runtime/<tenant_id>/chat/progress/ — no DRF auth classes; auth via X-NBHD-Internal-Key + X-NBHD-Tenant-Id headers validated by validate_internal_runtime_request (returns 401 internal_auth_failed on mismatch). Body: {"client_msg_id": "<optional>", "phase": "<required, max 24 chars>", "detail": "<optional, max 200 chars>"}. Returns 400 missing_fields if phase is absent. If client_msg_id is provided, updates that specific PENDING AppChatMessage. If omitted, updates the tenant's most-recently-created PENDING turn (the in-flight one). Finished (non-PENDING) turns are never mutated — returns {"updated": false} as a no-op. Unknown or absent turns also return 200 {"updated": false}. Fields phase and phase_detail on AppChatMessage are exposed on the polling endpoint GET /api/v1/chat/messages/<client_msg_id>/.
inventory_notes: Telegram/LINE turns do not create AppChatMessage rows, so a runtime hook firing during a Telegram turn has no row to update and silently no-ops. The per-tenant turn serialization (one container per tenant) is what makes 'most recent PENDING = in-flight' safe — there can only be one in-flight app turn at a time.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want my APNs token stored server-side and automatically pruned when it expires so that push notifications reliably reach my current device.
expected_behaviour: DeviceToken (db_table: device_tokens) stores one row per physical device install. Fields: token (globally unique, max 200 chars, hex), environment (sandbox|production), bundle_id, user FK, tenant FK, created_at, last_seen_at (auto-updated). Token uniqueness is enforced at DB level (UniqueConstraint on token). Self-healing: any push path (notify_app_reply_ready, notify_app_reply_error, notify_proactive_ready, PushTestView) collects APNs 410 / BadDeviceToken responses and deletes matching DeviceToken rows, so stale tokens from reinstall or token rotation are pruned automatically without a janitor cron.
inventory_notes: A sandbox and production token can coexist for one user (different environments). last_seen_at uses auto_now=True (updated on every save, including upsert). The global unique constraint on token means a device re-registration re-points the row to the new user atomically.
